value_post:
The #1 mistake travelers make: planning across 10 different apps.

Your itinerary is in Google Docs. Budget in a spreadsheet. Packing list on paper. Journal in your phone.

One Notion template can replace all of them.

Here's what a proper travel system looks like:

- Trip dashboard with countdowns
- Day-by-day itinerary builder
- Budget tracker with currency conversion
- Packing checklists by trip type
- Photo journal for every day
- Bucket list tracker

Stop losing travel info across 10 tabs.

thread_post:
I built the travel planning system I wish I had before my last trip.

It's a Notion template that handles everything from first idea to final memory.

Here's what's inside (thread):

1/ TRIP DASHBOARD

Your command center. See all upcoming trips with countdowns, quick links to every planning area, and your travel history at a glance.

One page to rule them all.

2/ BUDGET TRACKER WITH CURRENCY CONVERTER

Log expenses in any local currency. Set exchange rates. The template auto-calculates everything back to your home currency.

See spending by category, by day, or by total. Know instantly if you're on track or over budget.

3/ SMART PACKING CHECKLISTS

Pre-loaded lists for 5 trip types: beach, city, hiking, winter, business.

Check items off as you pack. Filter to see only unpacked items. Never forget your charger again.

4/ TRAVEL JOURNAL + PHOTO GALLERY

Capture daily entries with mood, weather, highlights, and photos.

Gallery view turns your journal into a beautiful visual timeline of your trip.

Available now for $12: https://tatsuya27.gumroad.com/l/travel-planner-journal
